description_long: This object consists of three rectangular stone grave markers arranged in a row, each with a slightly raised, weathered surface and beveled edges. The grave markers are made of dark, aged stone, showing signs of erosion and discoloration typical of old cemetery monuments. The tops of the markers are uneven, with subtle depressions and irregularities that suggest years of exposure to the elements. There are no visible inscriptions, engravings, or decorative motifs on the surfaces, giving the markers a somber and unadorned appearance. The bases of each marker are slightly wider than the tops, providing stability and a sense of permanence. The overall arrangement and material evoke a traditional, understated graveyard setting.
description: Three weathered, rectangular stone grave markers with beveled edges and uneven, aged surfaces, arranged in a row. The markers are dark, somber, and unadorned, evoking a traditional cemetery atmosphere.
description_view_0: A narrow, end-on view showing the three grave markers aligned in a row, emphasizing their rectangular shapes and the depth of each marker.
description_view_1: A frontal view displaying the full faces of all three grave markers, highlighting their weathered surfaces and uniform arrangement.
description_view_2: An opposite end-on view similar to view 0, showing the markers' alignment and the depth of each stone from the other side.
description_view_3: A rear view of the three grave markers, revealing the backs and bases, with the same weathered stone texture visible.
